member.name: Dr Kieran Mullan

addresses: 29-31 Sea Road, Bexhill on Sea, TN40 1EE

member.synopsis: Dr Kieran Mullan is the Conservative MP for Bexhill and Battle , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. He currently undertakes the role of Shadow Minister (Justice).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many offenders (broken down by type of offence) will be released at each of the scheduled early releases as part of the earned progression model as laid out by The Sentencing Act 2026 (Commencement No. 4) Reg
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government inherited a prisons system days from collapse. We had no choice but to take decisive action to stop our prisons overflowing and keep the public safe. Our landmark Sentencing Act 2026 will now deliver a more sustainable solution to the pris...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many victims have been sent letters advising them their perpetrator may be released from prison early as a result of the Sentencing Act 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government inherited a prison system on the brink of collapse, and we are fixing this crisis. Through the introduction of the landmark Sentencing Act, we are ensuring that courts retain the ability to impose custodial sentences on serious and dangero...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Crowborough Training Camp
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the value for money of using Crowborough Training Camp to accommodate asylum seekers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All large sites used for asylum accommodation are part of the Asylum Accommodation Programme which is part of the Government Major Projects Portfolio (GMPP) and, as such, are required to publish Accounting Officer Advice summaries on GOV.UK, with the most...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Care Homes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to reduce delays in Ofsted registration for new children’s homes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There has been a sustained and unprecedented rise in applications to register children’s homes, particularly in certain areas of the country such as the North West.In September 2025, in response to this rise Ofsted published their revised policy for prior...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Care Homes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what the average waiting time was for Ofsted registration of new children’s homes in each region in England in the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There has been a sustained and unprecedented rise in applications to register children’s homes, particularly in certain areas of the country such as the North West.In September 2025, in response to this rise Ofsted published their revised policy for prior...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Digital Technology: Babies and Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his policies of the findings of the research commissioned by the 1001 Critical Days Foundation on screen use among babies and children
writtenQuestions.answerText: The evidence available on the harms and benefits of screen use in under-five year olds is emerging and mixed. We welcome the research commissioned by the 1,001 Critical Days Foundation and its contribution to the evidence base.The early years are a critic...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Amateur Sports Clubs Scheme: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what recent assessment they have made of the adequacy of the existing membership fee threshold for participants in the CASC scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the important role that Community Amateur Sports Clubs (CASCs) play in supporting grassroots sport and promoting participation across the UK.CASC rules, including the threshold for annual membership fees, are designed to ensure c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what estimate he has made of the number of prisoners in custody serving standard determinate sentences, both in total, and broken down by offence, on the latest date on which the data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on prisoners serving determinate and indeterminate sentences are published regularly in the Offender Management Statistics Quarterly, which includes information on offenders by main custody type and offence group: https://www.gov.uk/government/collec...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Sports: Facilities
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to improve access to Government support for grassroots sports facilities; and what action it is taking to help increase the capital investment available for th
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government provides the majority of funding for grassroots sport through our Arm’s Length Body, Sport England – which annually invests over £250 million in Exchequer and Lottery funding in areas of greatest need to tackle inactivity levels through com...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Crown Prosecution Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, whether the CPS has guidance on whether and in what manner it should publicly comment on controversial public policy matters.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The CPS does not have any internal guidance relating to public commentary on public policy matters.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Attorney General's Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Livestock Industry: Migrant Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of a long term, dedicated visa route for overseas shearers to ensure timely shearing and protect livestock welfare.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of safe and timely shearing to protect animal welfare. The sheep shearing concession has been operating for 15 years and it is reasonable to expect that over this period a long-term sustainable solution had been fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Planning Permission: Enforcement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of delays related to the Planning Inspectorate’s processes of appeals against local authority enforcement notices have on the
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to the efficient handling of planning appeals, and the Planning Inspectorate has been working to improve processing and handling times in respect of them. As part of those efforts, it is trialling a pilot service of enforcement...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Geothermal Power: Skilled Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to support the redeployment of skills from the North Sea oil and gas sector into deep geothermal heat projects.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Across the energy system we are working to deliver new jobs in the clean energy transition and support those working in oil and gas to take up these opportunities. As part of our North Sea Future Plan we announced a North Sea Jobs Service will be launched...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Geothermal Power: Pride in Place Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether deep geothermal energy projects are eligible for support under the Neighbourhoods Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Neighbourhoods Fund, now known as the Pride in Place Programme, sits under the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, who is best placed to respond on eligibility. Heat networks can deliver local benefits by producing heat a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Geothermal Power: Warm Homes Plan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what role deep geothermal heat will play in delivering the Warm Homes Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In homes and buildings, a heat network is required to make use of deep geothermal heat. The Warm Homes Plan set out our support for low-carbon heat networks, including Heat Network Zoning and £1bn in investment over the course of this Parliament. It is fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Geothermal Power
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of of deep geothermal heat on long term energy security.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 2023, the Department co-funded a study with the Northeast Local Enterprise Partnership on the UK’s deep geothermal potential. Further research on the levelised costs of geothermal heat and power was published in summer 2025. Based on this evidence, the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Geothermal Power: Economic Growth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of deep geothermal energy on economic growth in coastal and post industrial communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 2023, the Department co-funded a study with the Northeast Local Enterprise Partnership on the UK’s deep geothermal potential. This outlines the opportunities and benefits of deep Geothermal energy in the UK, including how it can aid with the North Sea ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Geothermal Power
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to streamline planning and regulatory processes for deep geothermal heat developments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In homes and buildings, a heat network is required to make use of deep geothermal heat. The Warm Homes Plan set out our support for low-carbon heat networks, including Heat Network Zoning and £1bn in investment over the course of this Parliament. It is fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Geothermal Power
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to support the integration of geothermal heat into local heat networks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In homes and buildings, a heat network is required to make use of deep geothermal heat. The Warm Homes Plan set out our support for low-carbon heat networks, including Heat Network Zoning and £1bn in investment over the course of this Parliament. It is fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Geothermal Power
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of deep geothermal energy on the decarbonisation of NHS estates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We recognise the potential for deep geothermal energy to provide low‑carbon heat to parts of the National Health Service estate. However, opportunities depend on local geology, and a number of practical constraints need to be considered. The Department co...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
